known_limitations: The committed tests use per-case absolute tolerances and do not calculate a corpus-wide maximum relative error or ULP bound.
No new scalar safe path is frozen by this change; any future scalar promotion must add its own high-precision vectors and difficult-point assertions.

records.domain_set: small cancellation value; zero; exact cube; degree identity
records.max_abs_error: 1e-20 for log1p(1e-8); 1e-15 for the remaining asserted values
records.status: reference assertions passed on the reviewed GNU MinGW profile

records.domain_set: ordinary values, identities, singular-domain rejection, and contained native error paths
records.operation: gamma / beta / error / Airy / exponential and logarithmic integrals
records.status: reference and contract assertions passed on the reviewed GNU MinGW profile

records.comparator: pi identities and exact unit cases
records.domain_set: identity values, invalid signs, native range status, and post-error reuse
records.operation: Spence and Carlson symmetric elliptic integrals
records.status: reference and native-status assertions passed on the reviewed GNU MinGW profile

records.domain_set: ordinary valid execution case for each exposed wrapper
records.max_abs_error: not measured by the committed f32 smoke test
records.status: execution coverage only; no f32 accuracy superiority claim

scope: No safe scalar path is introduced by this release-candidate change. This report makes the existing reviewed scalar reference coverage and its limits explicit rather than inventing aggregate accuracy claims.
